name: seed_bulk_500_mega_13
query:
BEGIN;
INSERT INTO public.questions
(id, question_text, stimulus_text, stimulus_type, options, correct_answer, explanation, hint, topic, subtopic, section, domain_id, skill_id, math_skill_code, difficulty, calculator_allowed, exam_type, question_type, is_platform_question, ai_generated, source, source_metadata, created_at, updated_at)
VALUES ('75d3e453-aa4d-4586-843b-23f14413a895', 'Which statement is best supported by the poll?', 'A poll of 1,000 voters reports 45% support for a measure, with a margin of error of ±4 percentage points at a 95% confidence level.', NULL, '[{"id":"A","text":"Support could plausibly be as low as 41% or as high as 49%","is_correct":true,"explanation":"The interval is 41% to 49%."},{"id":"B","text":"Exactly 45% of all voters in the country support the measure","is_correct":false,"explanation":"A sample estimate is not an exact population value."},{"id":"C","text":"The poll proves every voter was surveyed","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Only 1,000 voters were polled."},{"id":"D","text":"Margin of error means the poll is meaningless","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Margin of error quantifies uncertainty; it does not invalidate the poll."}]'::jsonb, 'A', 'With ±4 points, plausible support ranges from 41% to 49%.', NULL, 'Problem-Solving and Data Analysis', 'Inference from sample statistics', 'math', 'psda', 'IEE', 'IEE', 'hard', true, 'SAT', 'multiple_choice', true, true, 'scholaris_ai', '{"generator":"scholaris_bulk_v1","original":true,"explanation_v2":true}'::jsonb, now(), now())
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
INSERT INTO public.questions
(id, question_text, stimulus_text, stimulus_type, options, correct_answer, explanation, hint, topic, subtopic, section, domain_id, skill_id, math_skill_code, difficulty, calculator_allowed, exam_type, question_type, is_platform_question, ai_generated, source, source_metadata, created_at, updated_at)
VALUES ('fdf3d32e-5647-4388-8622-11354e31e527', 'Which statement is best supported by the poll?', 'A poll of 1,000 voters reports 46% support for a measure, with a margin of error of ±5 percentage points at a 95% confidence level.', NULL, '[{"id":"A","text":"Support could plausibly be as low as 41% or as high as 51%","is_correct":true,"explanation":"The interval is 41% to 51%."},{"id":"B","text":"Exactly 46% of all voters in the country support the measure","is_correct":false,"explanation":"A sample estimate is not an exact population value."},{"id":"C","text":"The poll proves every voter was surveyed","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Only 1,000 voters were polled."},{"id":"D","text":"Margin of error means the poll is meaningless","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Margin of error quantifies uncertainty; it does not invalidate the poll."}]'::jsonb, 'A', 'With ±5 points, plausible support ranges from 41% to 51%.', NULL, 'Problem-Solving and Data Analysis', 'Inference from sample statistics', 'math', 'psda', 'IEE', 'IEE', 'hard', true, 'SAT', 'multiple_choice', true, true, 'scholaris_ai', '{"generator":"scholaris_bulk_v1","original":true,"explanation_v2":true}'::jsonb, now(), now())
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
INSERT INTO public.questions
(id, question_text, stimulus_text, stimulus_type, options, correct_answer, explanation, hint, topic, subtopic, section, domain_id, skill_id, math_skill_code, difficulty, calculator_allowed, exam_type, question_type, is_platform_question, ai_generated, source, source_metadata, created_at, updated_at)
VALUES ('433bbb99-67a4-4127-b2db-40c6883d8332', 'Which choice best evaluates the blogger''s claim?', 'A blogger writes, "Everyone in the city opposes the new bike lane," after surveying 60 people at one weekend farmers market.', NULL, '[{"id":"A","text":"The claim is valid because 50 people is a large sample","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Sample size alone does not fix selection bias."},{"id":"B","text":"The claim is valid because farmers markets represent all residents","is_correct":false,"explanation":"One location on one weekend is not citywide representation."},{"id":"C","text":"The claim is valid because weekend opinions never change","is_correct":false,"explanation":"No evidence supports this absolutist statement."},{"id":"D","text":"The claim is overstated because the sample is not representative of the whole city","is_correct":true,"explanation":"Convenience samples cannot support ''everyone'' claims."}]'::jsonb, 'D', 'Generalizing from a single venue to ''everyone in the city'' overreaches the evidence.', NULL, 'Problem-Solving and Data Analysis', 'Evaluating statistical claims', 'math', 'psda', 'EVA', 'EVA', 'easy', true, 'SAT', 'multiple_choice', true, true, 'scholaris_ai', '{"generator":"scholaris_bulk_v1","original":true,"explanation_v2":true}'::jsonb, now(), now())
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

VALUES ('3c968789-2251-4794-b6b5-052dbc1d4c8b', 'Which choice best evaluates the blogger''s claim?', 'A blogger writes, "Everyone in the city opposes the new bike lane," after surveying 90 people at one weekend farmers market.', NULL, '[{"id":"A","text":"The claim is valid because 50 people is a large sample","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Sample size alone does not fix selection bias."},{"id":"B","text":"The claim is valid because farmers markets represent all residents","is_correct":false,"explanation":"One location on one weekend is not citywide representation."},{"id":"C","text":"The claim is valid because weekend opinions never change","is_correct":false,"explanation":"No evidence supports this absolutist statement."},{"id":"D","text":"The claim is overstated because the sample is not representative of the whole city","is_correct":true,"explanation":"Convenience samples cannot support ''everyone'' claims."}]'::jsonb, 'D', 'Generalizing from a single venue to ''everyone in the city'' overreaches the evidence.', NULL, 'Problem-Solving and Data Analysis', 'Evaluating statistical claims', 'math', 'psda', 'EVA', 'EVA', 'hard', true, 'SAT', 'multiple_choice', true, true, 'scholaris_ai', '{"generator":"scholaris_bulk_v1","original":true,"explanation_v2":true}'::jsonb, now(), now())
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

VALUES ('9f73325d-bc0f-465a-be7c-91b0c5c16a20', 'What is the measure of angle $C$, in degrees?', 'In triangle $ABC$, the measure of angle $A$ is 96° and the measure of angle $B$ is 60°.', NULL, '[{"id":"A","text":"$96°$","is_correct":false,"explanation":"That is angle A."},{"id":"B","text":"$60°$","is_correct":false,"explanation":"That is angle B."},{"id":"C","text":"$106°$","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Angles must sum to 180°; this is too large."},{"id":"D","text":"$24°$","is_correct":true,"explanation":"$96° + 60° + 24° = 180°$."}]'::jsonb, 'D', 'Triangle angle sum: $m\angle C = 180 - 96 - 60 = 24°$.', NULL, 'Geometry and Trigonometry', 'Lines, angles, and triangles', 'math', 'geometry', 'LIN_G', 'LIN_G', 'medium', true, 'SAT', 'multiple_choice', true, true, 'scholaris_ai', '{"generator":"scholaris_bulk_v1","original":true,"explanation_v2":true}'::jsonb, now(), now())

VALUES ('2c6fe393-0176-4ed6-8352-f5d9f28ec4f3', 'What is the length of the hypotenuse?', 'A right triangle has legs of length 13 and 14.', NULL, '[{"id":"A","text":"$18$","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Too short for the hypotenuse."},{"id":"B","text":"$27$","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Hypotenuse is not the sum of the legs."},{"id":"C","text":"$19$","is_correct":true,"explanation":"$c = \\sqrt{13^2 + 14^2} = 19$."},{"id":"D","text":"$21$","is_correct":false,"explanation":"Too long."}]'::jsonb, 'C', 'Pythagorean theorem: $c = \sqrt{13^2 + 14^2} = 19$.', NULL, 'Geometry and Trigonometry', 'Right triangles and trigonometry', 'math', 'geometry', 'RIG', 'RIG', 'easy', true, 'SAT', 'multiple_choice', true, true, 'scholaris_ai', '{"generator":"scholaris_bulk_v1","original":true,"explanation_v2":true}'::jsonb, now(), now())

(Use $\pi \approx 3.14$.)', 'A circle has center $O$ and radius 4.', NULL, '[{"id":"A","text":"$50.24$","is_correct":false,"explanation":"That is an area expression $\\pi r^2$, not circumference."},{"id":"B","text":"$25.12$","is_correct":true,"explanation":"Circumference $C = 2\\pi r \\approx 2(3.14)(4) = 25.12$."},{"id":"C","text":"$8.00$","is_correct":false,"explanation":"That is the diameter, not circumference."},{"id":"D","text":"$12.56$","is_correct":false,"explanation":"That is $\\pi r$, not $2\\pi r$."}]'::jsonb, 'B', '$C = 2\pi r \approx 2(3.14)(4) = 25.12$.', NULL, 'Geometry and Trigonometry', 'Circles', 'math', 'geometry', 'CIR', 'CIR', 'easy', true, 'SAT', 'multiple_choice', true, true, 'scholaris_ai', '{"generator":"scholaris_bulk_v1","original":true,"explanation_v2":true}'::jsonb, now(), now())
